texto: When the battery in my watch died, I still wore it. There was something about the watch that said: It doesn’t matter what time it is. Think in months. Years. Someone loves you. Where are you going? There are some things you will never do. It doesn’t matter. There is no rush. Be the best prisoner you can be.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Chris Huntington que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “When the battery in my watch died, I still wore it. There was something about the watch that said…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, life-lessons, memory, com foco em memory e time.
